cent: noun. A subunit of currency equal to one-hundredth of the main unit of currency in many countries.

con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
cone: noun. A surface of revolution formed by rotating a segment of a line around another line that intersects the first line.

cop: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Coptic.
cope: verb. To deal effectively with something, especially if difficult.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
men: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mende (Sierra Leone).

mon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mongolian.

mote: noun. A small particle; a speck.
motet: noun. A composition adapted to sacred words in the elaborate polyphonic church style; an anthem.
mott: noun. A copse or small grove of trees, especially live oak or elm.
motte: noun. A raised earth mound, often topped with a wooden or stone structure and surrounded with a ditch.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nett: adjective. Alternative spelling of net (remaining after expenses or deductions).
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
octet: noun. A group or set of eight of something.

once: adverb. One and only one time.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ope: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Old Persian.
opt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Opata.
pec: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Pesisir.
pen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Penesak.
peon: noun. A lowly person; a peasant or serf; a labourer who is obliged to do menial work.
pet: noun. An animal kept as a companion or otherwise for pleasure, rather than for some practical benefit or use.
petto: noun. Only used in in petto

pome: noun. A type of fruit in which the often edible flesh arises from the swollen base of the flower and not from the carpels.

pone: noun. A writ in law used by the superior courts to remove cases from inferior courts.
pot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Potawatomi.
tempt: verb. To provoke someone to do wrong, especially by promising a reward; to entice.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tet: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tetum.
